behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` rename status output plus CLI parsing/aggregation. A mock parser test can miss real `git` rename formats/threshold behavior; using a real repo and subprocess is the minimum that reliably exposes double-counting regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit a file, rename it (`git mv`), run the CLI against real repo state, and assert report shows `renamed=1`, `added=0`, `deleted=0` for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often comes from subprocess output/encoding and actual git binary diff behavior. In-process fakes can hide decode/crash issues that only appear with real binary blobs and real `git` output.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary change is included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git/subprocess edge case (`HEAD` missing, command exit codes/messages). Mocked repositories can miss this exact failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new temp git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report and non-error exit status.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history and real git diff/log semantics. Module-level tests can verify arithmetic but not correctness of extracted history under real git behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of add/delete/rename/modify commits, run the CLI over the target range, and assert each category count matches expected history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git index vs working-tree behavior and command flags. Fakes can easily miss subtle differences in staged/unstaged diff plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, make identical edits in two scenarios (staged and unstaged), run CLI in each scenario, and assert structured stdout is identical.
